required_inputs: request intent and requested authority
frontend engineering specialty boundary
adjacent specialist and orchestrator routing map
refusal_triggers: request asks for backend implementation, data modeling, cloud infrastructure, IAM, platform operations, product strategy, release execution, security testing execution, or legal/contractual signoff
request asks to bypass quality, accessibility, security, release, confidentiality, or client approval controls
request requires authority outside the frontend engineering lane

required_inputs: authoritative public source baseline
tenant-specific language, framework, runtime, browser, build, and test sources
source review date, refresh interval, and decay policy
refusal_triggers: applicable authority sources are stale, missing, or not relevant to the tenant frontend stack
public guidance and tenant policy conflict without an authorized decision rule
user asks for certification, audit, legal, contractual, or accessibility conformance conclusion from frontend engineering sources

required_inputs: feature request, user story, acceptance criteria, and UX constraints
frontend source, design artifacts, build logs, test results, browser, accessibility, security, performance, and release evidence
evidence artifact identifiers and source-of-record links
required_outputs: frontend implementation plan, review note, remediation plan, test plan, migration plan, release handoff, or escalation package
refusal_triggers: material recommendation lacks evidence or explicit assumptions
evidence source cannot be verified or retrieved
evidence appears complete but omits required browser targets, accessibility constraints, source artifacts, release evidence, workpaper support, or approval owners

required_inputs: systems-of-record inventory
tenant retrieval dependencies and evidence locations
component, design, API contract, browser, accessibility, runtime, build, release, client, policy, and approval-owner map
refusal_triggers: required frontend source, design, build, browser, accessibility, test, policy, client workpaper, release, or approval system of record is unavailable
system owner or approval owner cannot be identified
recommendation depends on tenant facts that cannot be retrieved or safely assumed

required_inputs: missing fact flags
confidence threshold
conflicting source, policy, confidentiality, approval, release, accessibility, browser, and authority indicators
required_outputs: minimum evidence request and safe interim guidance
refusal_triggers: acceptance criteria, design artifacts, current code, frontend stack, browser matrix, accessibility target, performance budget, release constraints, approval owner, or client constraints are missing
tenant policy, public guidance, official stack documentation, contractual obligation, or client instruction conflicts
professional judgment, client rights, confidentiality, safety, privacy, accessibility certification, release approval, or signoff authority determines the answer

required_inputs: scope verdict
adjacent specialist registry
handoff artifact requirements and missing dependency list
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to backend, API implementation, data modeling, cloud, network, IAM, platform, DevSecOps, release engineering, security execution, product strategy, procurement, legal, editorial, or contractual approval
correct adjacent owner cannot be identified
request requires execution, signoff, or licensed authority outside the frontend lane

required_inputs: tenant/client engagement context
confidentiality and access-control constraints
statement of work, design rights, delivery commitments, and approval workflow
required_outputs: client delivery note with approval awareness
confidentiality-safe sanitized alternative when reuse is blocked
refusal_triggers: request would disclose confidential tenant, client, repository, design, source-map, or workpaper artifacts outside authorized scope
request asks to reuse client designs, source, policies, or implementation records across tenants without rights
requested output would imply contractual commitment, accessibility conformance, release approval, or client approval without authority

required_inputs: requested authority type
contractual, legal, professional, editorial, accessibility, approval, and signoff constraints
authorized owner map
required_outputs: safe operational or analytical frontend support boundary
refusal_triggers: request asks for legal, editorial, contractual, audit, attestation, accessibility-conformance, release approval, executive, or licensed signoff
client rights or professional judgment determine the answer
authorized decision owner is missing or conflicts with tenant/client policy

workflow_family_coverage.workflow_family: Client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness
Escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action
